steps.title: Pin down the goal
steps.content: The student wants to describe the rocking chair to an audience that does not know Sam Maloof. So the sentence must (1) identify Maloof as the maker and (2) describe the chair itself.

steps.title: Look for a choice that does both
steps.content: Only one option names Maloof (and what he did) while also describing the chair's physical features pulled from the notes (sleek, contoured armrests and seat; walnut; held by Boston's Museum of Fine Arts).

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'sleek, contoured armrests and seat, the walnut rocking chair...American woodworker Sam Maloof': identifies Maloof for the unfamiliar audience and describes the chair using relevant notes. ✓
  • Choice B — 'born in 1916 and died in 2009...a chair that you can see': gives biographical dates but no description of the chair (overreach on biography, misread of the goal). ✗
  • Choice C — 'recipient of one of the...genius grants': focuses on an award and never describes the chair at all (misread of the goal). ✗
  • Choice D — 'made from walnut...sleek and contoured': describes the chair but never names Maloof, so the unfamiliar audience is not introduced to him (misread of the audience constraint). ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
